sourceDrugName: Paracetamol 10 mg/mL, 100 mL Solution for Injection Vial
commonUses: Commonly used for fever and mild to moderate pain relief.
warnings: Avoid taking more than the recommended label dose. Ask a pharmacist or doctor before use if you have liver disease, heavy alcohol use, or are taking other products that also contain paracetamol/acetaminophen.
sideEffects: Side effects are uncommon at recommended doses, but serious allergic reactions or liver injury can occur with overdose or unsafe combinations.
contraindications: See official product label and healthcare professional guidance.
drugInteractions: Check with a pharmacist or doctor before combining medicines.
storageInstructions: Store at room temperature, away from heat and moisture.
safetyNotes: Do not use this dataset for diagnosis, prescription, or dosing decisions. Verify against product label/FDA registration and pharmacist/doctor advice.

sourceDrugName: Ibuprofen 100 mg/5 mL, 60 mL Oral Suspension
commonUses: Commonly used for pain, inflammation, and fever relief.
warnings: May irritate the stomach and may not be suitable for people with stomach ulcers, kidney disease, certain heart conditions, bleeding risk, or NSAID allergy. Ask a healthcare professional if pregnant or taking blood thinners.
sideEffects: Possible stomach upset, heartburn, dizziness, allergic reaction, and increased bleeding risk.
contraindications: See official product label and healthcare professional guidance.
drugInteractions: Check with a pharmacist or doctor before combining medicines.
storageInstructions: Store at room temperature, away from heat and moisture.
safetyNotes: Do not use this dataset for diagnosis, prescription, or dosing decisions. Verify against product label/FDA registration and pharmacist/doctor advice.

sourceDrugName: Cetirizine 1 mg/mL, 60 mL Oral Solution Bottle
commonUses: Commonly used for allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes, or hives.
warnings: May cause drowsiness in some people. Use caution when driving or operating machinery until you know how it affects you.
sideEffects: Possible sleepiness, dry mouth, tiredness, or dizziness.

drugInteractions: Check with a pharmacist or doctor before combining medicines.
